config.exam_name: 27 DLT Internals & Incremental load

question.line: What is one of the biggest advantages of Delta Live Tables (DLT) regarding pipeline datasets?

options.A: Developers must manually create and delete every Delta table.
options.B: DLT automatically manages the lifecycle of datasets created within a pipeline.
options.C: DLT stores all datasets only in local notebook memory.
options.D: Developers must manually register every table in Unity Catalog.
options.E: DLT automatically converts all tables into streaming tables.

question.line: What is created each time a Delta Live Tables pipeline executes?

options.C: An Update containing execution status, logs, metrics, and Spark UI information
options.E: A second pipeline for disaster recovery

question.line: When 10,000 new rows are added to the orders_raw table and the DLT pipeline is rerun, what happens?

options.A: The entire Orders and Customers datasets are reprocessed.
options.B: Only the Customers Bronze table is refreshed.
options.C: Only the new 10,000 order records are processed by the Streaming Table, and downstream layers are updated automatically.
options.D: The Gold table must be rebuilt manually.
options.E: All checkpoints are deleted before processing begins.

question.line: Why are Streaming Tables efficient for incremental ETL?

options.A: They always reload every record from the source.
options.B: They process only new data by remembering previously processed records through checkpoints.
options.C: They permanently cache all source data in memory.
options.D: They require manual filtering of duplicate records.

question.line: What happens to Materialized Views when upstream data changes?

options.A: They must be manually refreshed by the developer.
options.C: They automatically recompute joins and aggregations to produce the latest results.
options.D: They stop accepting new data until the schema is recreated.
options.E: They are converted into temporary views.

question.line: How does Delta Live Tables handle schema evolution when a developer renames a column or adds a new aggregation?

options.A: The developer must manually alter the destination table before running the pipeline.
options.B: The schema cannot be changed after the pipeline is created.
options.C: Only the transformation code needs to be updated, and DLT automatically applies the schema changes during execution.
options.D: The existing pipeline must be deleted and recreated.
options.E: Schema changes are supported only for Streaming Tables.

question.line: What happens when a dataset such as joined_silver is renamed to order_silver in the DLT notebook?

options.A: Both datasets remain in Unity Catalog permanently.
options.B: Developers must manually delete the old dataset after the pipeline completes.
options.C: DLT automatically removes the old dataset, creates the new one, and updates pipeline metadata.
options.D: The pipeline fails because dataset names cannot be changed.
options.E: Only the notebook name changes; the dataset name remains the same.

question.line: Where is the physical data for DLT Streaming Tables and Materialized Views actually stored?

options.C: In hidden Delta tables within the Databricks Internal Catalog managed by DLT
options.D: Only in Unity Catalog metadata tables

question.line: What information is stored in Streaming Table checkpoints?

options.A: User login credentials and cluster passwords
options.C: Processed records, stream offsets, and processing progress used for incremental execution

question.line: Which statement about Unity Catalog Lineage is correct?

options.A: It is available only for Delta Live Tables pipelines.
options.B: It tracks only table names and ignores columns.
options.C: It provides end-to-end table and column-level lineage for DLT pipelines and other Unity Catalog-managed assets.
options.D: It works only for Streaming Tables.
options.E: It requires developers to manually document every transformation.
